preamble: The loss of a final glottal stop [ʔ] is a foundational mechanism in Tonogenesis, specifically in the development of rising tones. According to Haudricourt's model of Vietnamese and similar processes in Sinitic languages, the disappearance of this coda triggers a compensatory rise in pitch on the preceding vowel.
phoneticEffects: Tonogenesis, Coda Loss, Rising Tone, Haudricourt's Model

examples.note: The loss of final glottal stops led to the development of the rising tones.
The Shǎng (rising) tone developed from an original glottal coda.

sources: Haudricourt, A. G. (1954). De l'origine des tons en viêtnamien.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
